assumption_to_test: a canonical active release manifest and drift audit can keep self-hosting release refs synchronized without manual JSON editing
smallest_testable_validation: add one active release manifest, route organization-status and contract-register through it, and verify that post-release drift is detected on the current self-hosting repo
expected_learning: whether release-state freshness is best solved by a single manifest plus audit, or whether broader lifecycle writebacks are required
expected_cost: one narrow runtime slice plus verification updates
success_threshold: active runtime surfaces converge on one release baseline and a drift case can be reproduced as a benchmark
